disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Grouped blisters on a red base with a preceding tingling/burning, near the eye
trigger_question_examples: Are they grouped little blisters on red skin, and did you get tingling/burning first?
How close to the eye are they?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, grouped blisters on red skin, and I had tingling and burning for a couple of days first; they're just below my eye.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Screen for ocular involvement: red/painful eye, light sensitivity, gritty/foreign-body feeling, blurred vision, blisters on the eyelid margin or tip of the nose
trigger_question_examples: Any red or painful eye, sensitivity to light, a gritty feeling, or blurred vision?
Are there any blisters on your eyelid edge or the tip of your nose?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No red or painful eye, no light sensitivity or grittiness, vision is normal, and no blisters on my eyelid or nose tip.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: My eye feels okay I think.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Precipitated by stress and recent UV/sun exposure; recurrent cold sores; not immunosuppressed
trigger_question_examples: Any triggers like stress or sun, and do you get recurrent cold sores?
Any immune problems or medications that lower immunity?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I've been stressed and had a lot of sun recently, and I get cold sores now and then; no immune problems.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this going to get into my eye and make me go blind?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Take the ocular risk seriously: screen for ocular symptoms, start antivirals promptly, advise strict hand hygiene to avoid autoinoculation, arrange urgent (next-day) in-person review, and give a clear ocular safety-net. Should not falsely reassure that the eye is definitely fine (cannot be confirmed remotely) nor dismiss the concern.
